sections.description: High-level product identity, mission statement, core purposes, unique positioning among DF tools, roadmap summary, phase dependencies, estimated timeline, and reference repository inventory.

subsections.description: Chronicler's mission, the two core purposes (historical record + live advisor), and its unique position relative to existing DF tools.
7-phase roadmap summary, dependency chain between phases, and high-level timeline estimates.
Catalog of 17+ third-party repositories studied during research (df-ai, df-narrator, weblegends, DwarfFortressLogger, dfhack-client-python, etc.) with relevance notes.

sections.description: Overall system architecture including the six main components (MC-1 through MC-6), two foundational systems (FS-1 CDM, FS-2 ETL), cross-cutting concerns, data flow between layers, and feature inventory summary.

subsections.description: Diagram and list of all six main components (Visualizer, Explorer, Storyteller, Advisor, Mod Manager, Labor Manager) and two foundational systems.
End-to-end data flow: DF game -> DFHack bridge -> ETL pipeline -> PostgreSQL CDM -> API -> UI components.
Consolidated table of all features across all components with priority and phase assignments.

sections.description: PostgreSQL 16 database schema design: composite PKs, JSONB columns, complete table set (40+ tables), missing entity types, HF field extensions, importance score columns, knowledge horizon table, worldgen snapshots, world modpacks, and pgvector embedding tables.

subsections.description: PostgreSQL 16 with PostGIS, pg_trgm, unaccent, pgvector. Connection pooling, ParadeDB/pg_search extensions.
Composite PKs (world_id, entity_id), JSONB details columns for flexible data, event types stored as TEXT strings.
All 40+ CDM tables covering historical figures, entities, sites, regions, artifacts, events, written content, structures, populations, etc. Includes coverage comparison against reference repos.
Entity types not yet in CDM (dancing forms, musical forms, poetic forms, etc.) and HF field extensions needed beyond baseline. Full HF field completeness audit.
Additional tables for importance scores, knowledge horizon tracking, worldgen parameter snapshots, world modpack records, and pgvector entity embeddings.

sections.description: All data ingestion and transformation systems: Legends XML streaming parser, dual-file merge, 14+ XML section handling, 10-step post-parse pipeline, live DFHack bridge, change detection, file transfer, and RAG/vector indexing.

subsections.description: Streaming SAX/iterparse XML parser handling legends.xml and legends_plus.xml dual-file merge, parsing all 14+ XML sections.
10-step post-parse pipeline: relationship resolution, importance scoring, NVS computation, rivalry detection, family chain construction, embedding generation, etc.

DFHack Lua bridge (v6) providing real-time game data via HTTP JSON server. Core bridge plus enhancements for eventful subscriptions, death cause enrichment, family chain, book detection, personality/soul data, and skill progression.
Subscribe to DFHack eventful module for real-time in-game event capture.

aliases: REQ-ETL-007: Bridge Enhancement -- Death Cause Enrichment
subsections.description: Enrich death events with full cause detail from DFHack unit data.
Traverse and serialize full family relationship chains from DFHack unit/HF data.
Detect written content (books, scrolls) in fortress inventory via bridge.
Export unit soul/personality facet data (50 facets) via bridge.
Export unit skill levels and experience values via bridge.
worldgen-bridge.lua Lua script that monitors world generation progress and captures parameter snapshots.
11 live change event types detected and dispatched by the bridge/watcher system (death, birth, migration, etc.).
VM-to-host file transfer via HTTP server (port 8889, ~105 MB/s) and SCP fallback. Used to retrieve legends exports from Windows VM.
SSH-based dfhack-run transport for executing Lua commands remotely on the Windows VM. Replaces broken TCP RPC for game-thread calls.
pgvector-based entity embedding and semantic search indexing pipeline. Enables similarity search over historical figures, events, and narratives.

sections.description: Interactive world map, demographic charts, event timelines, genealogy trees, network graphs, and all other data visualizations for the historical record.

subsections.description: Leaflet.js-based interactive world map with zoom/pan on the DF world PNG image.
Toggleable layer groups for sites, civilizations, regions, trade routes, etc.

aliases: REQ-VIS-003: Site Marker Shapes by Type
subsections.description: Distinct marker shapes per site type (fortress, city, cave, etc.) on the map.
Consistent per-civilization color coding applied across map markers, charts, and network graphs.
Small embedded map widget on entity detail pages showing the entity's location(s).
Draggable year-range slider that animates map state (site ownership, civilization extent) across historical time.
Polygon overlays on the map showing territorial extent of civilizations at a given point in time.
Real-time preview of the world map as it is being generated by DF (worldgen phase).
Search box that pans and zooms the map to a named site or entity location.
Visual bounding rectangles on the map for sites with known coordinate extents.
Population doughnut/pie charts, event count line chart over time, event type bar chart breakdown. Covers demographic analytics.
D3.js war chord diagram, force-directed warfare graph, vis.js entity relationship graph. Covers all inter-entity network visualizations.
Family tree rendering for historical figures and curse lineage trees for supernatural entities.
Landing dashboard with aggregate world statistics: population totals, event counts, civilization count, notable figures.
Browseable list of historical eras, year-by-year event browser, and hierarchical event collection viewer.
Sparkline or mini-chart widget showing real-time data curves (population, stress, etc.) sourced from the live bridge.

aliases: REQ-VIS-011: Hover Popovers (Map and All Entity Links)
subsections.description: Tooltip/popover UI on map markers and all entity hyperlinks showing a summary card on hover.

sections.description: Schema browser, data grid browser, entity detail pages, full-text search, advanced query builder, SQL runner, and data export tools.

subsections.description: Table list with row counts, column/type/PK/FK/index detail, visual FK relationship lines, and JSONB column field inventory.
Paginated data grid with column-level filtering, column sorting, FK link navigation, JSONB collapsible expansion, and row detail overlay.

Rich detail pages for all entity types: Historical Figure (24 sections), Entity/Civilization (5 tabs), Site (3 tabs), Artifact, Region, Structure, Written Content, Event Collection, plus additional entity types.

aliases: REQ-EXP-021: Global Search with Live Autocomplete
REQ-EXP-022: HF Filtering by Type Flags
subsections.description: Accent-insensitive full-text search (pg_trgm/unaccent), global search with live autocomplete, and Historical Figure filtering by type flags.
Visual query builder with drag-and-drop conditions and a raw SQL runner for power users.

aliases: REQ-EXP-026: Entity Data Export (Full Entity JSON)
subsections.description: Export query results as CSV/JSON and export full entity records as JSON blobs.
Inline hyperlinks between entity types, perspective-aware event text, breadcrumb trails, prev/next navigation within lists, and per-entity ego-network graph.

sections.description: LLM-powered conversational world Q&A, agentic SQL tool use, event narrative template system, character biography generation, war chronicles, template/LLM hybrid rendering, SSE streaming, monitoring dashboard, and proactive narrative engine.

subsections.description: LLM chat interface for asking questions about the DF world. Dual-tier context (historical legends + live bridge data), SSE streaming, multi-world support.
LLM agent with SQL tool use, able to autonomously query the CDM to answer complex questions about world history.
LLM must signal confidence levels and clearly state when no record exists rather than hallucinating.

aliases: REQ-STR-016: Per-Type Event Narrative Templates (132+ types)
REQ-STR-021: Interaction Text from Game Raws
subsections.description: Template-driven rendering for 132+ event types with 50+ death cause variants, circumstance/reason rendering, temporal context, game raws interaction text, fallback for unknown events, and event payload enrichment.

aliases: REQ-STR-009: Unified Person View (Unit + HF Merge)
REQ-STR-011: Relationship Traversal on HF Match
REQ-STR-025: Narrative Value Score (NVS) for Denizens
REQ-STR-027: Top-N Entity Selection for LLM Context
subsections.description: Biography generation, unit+HF unified person view, personality-driven voice emulation, relationship traversal, emotion/zone integration, NVS scoring, rivalry detection, and top-N entity selection for LLM context window.
Generated prose narratives for wars, individual battles, and full civilization rise-and-fall arcs.

aliases: REQ-STR-030: Template vs. LLM Hybrid Rendering
subsections.description: Decision logic for when to use deterministic Jinja templates vs. LLM generation, balancing quality, latency, and cost.
LLM provider configuration (model selection, temperature, max tokens), per-interaction logging, and a monitoring dashboard showing latency, token usage, and error rates.
Format all timestamps using the DF in-game calendar (months, seasons, years) consistently across narrative output.
Future features: proactive narrative push (LLM-initiated story events), and deep integration with Knowledge Horizon scope masking.

sections.description: Reactive AI control architecture for autonomous fortress management or advisory mode: polling, population cycle, action queue, military sizing, resource management, construction planning, trade cycle, threat assessment, and LLM-powered advice.

aliases: REQ-ADV-005: Advisor vs. Autonomous Mode Toggle
subsections.description: Tick-based multi-rate polling loop, 10-phase population update cycle, reactive control architecture, exclusive action queue preventing conflicting actions, and advisor vs. autonomous mode toggle.
Citizen arrival/departure tracking, noble position assignment recommendations, job stall detection and alerting.
Advisor recommendations for military force size relative to population and threat level, plus squad creation and sizing actions.
Three-tier stock thresholds (critical/low/target), automatic production order placement, farm plot management, and metalworking production chain management.
Automated construction planning for 22 room types based on population needs and available space.
9-step trade cycle automation: caravan detection, goods selection, negotiation, and post-trade inventory reconciliation.
Threat assessment heuristics, fortress health summary, event-driven reactive alerts (goblin siege, tantrum spiral, etc.), and context-aware proactive alerts.
Natural language fortress advice, multi-step action plans generated by LLM, and fortress post-mortem narrative generation after fortress death.
Embark site scoring and evaluation, plus random embark with automatic world restart for unattended play.

sections.description: Full mod lifecycle management: discovery, info.txt parsing, modpack CRUD, profile import/export, load order management, multi-level conflict detection, raw file tokenizer, three-way merge, vanilla baseline management, full raw compiler, modpack snapshot, Steam Workshop integration, and CLI interface.

aliases: REQ-MOD-001: Mod Discovery via Filesystem Scan
subsections.description: Filesystem scan for installed mods, live mod discovery via DFHack, and info.txt parser covering all DF v50 metadata fields.
Create/read/update/delete modpacks, import/export profiles as JSON, manual and LOOT-style automatic load order management.
Three-tier conflict detection: Level 1 (metadata incompatibility), Level 2 (object ID collision), Level 3 (semantic raw conflicts). Visual conflict indicators in UI.
DF raw file tokenizer/parser, three-way merge of conflicting raw files, vanilla baseline snapshot management, and full raw compiler producing merged output.

aliases: REQ-MOD-016: Modpack Snapshot at World Creation
REQ-MOD-018: Mod Annotation in Legends Display
subsections.description: Snapshot active modpack at world creation, store mod history in CDM, annotate legends/entity displays with mod-of-origin information.
Browse and install mods from Steam Workshop. CLI interface for headless/scripted mod management operations.

sections.description: Dwarf Therapist-style labor management: toggle grid, skill display, personality visualization, happiness/stress monitoring, squad assignment, noble management, profession management, filtering, thought/emotion display, need satisfaction, attribute display, citizen roster polling, AI labor advisor, skill-based auto-assignment, migration tracking, job management, wound tracking, inventory display, relationship visualization, skill analytics, stress trends, batch operations, and optimization engine.

aliases: REQ-LAB-012: Citizen Roster with Configurable Polling
subsections.description: Labor toggle grid (dwarf x labor matrix), configurable polling citizen roster displaying all current dwarves with live data.
Per-dwarf skill levels and progression bars, physical/mental attribute scores, and fortress-wide skill distribution analytics.
50-facet personality trait visualization, happiness/stress score monitoring, thought and emotion display, need satisfaction tracking, and stress trend analysis over time.
Squad assignment UI, noble/position holder management, and custom profession label management.
Multi-criteria dwarf filtering and sorting, plus batch labor enable/disable operations across groups.
LLM-powered labor assignment recommendations, skill-based automatic labor assignment, and a labor optimization engine that maximizes production given workforce constraints.

aliases: REQ-LAB-016: Job Management and Stall Detection
subsections.description: Immigrant arrival/wave tracking, job stall detection, newcomer orientation view for recently arrived dwarves, and deathwatch/casualty log.
Per-dwarf wound and health status tracking, inventory and equipment display, and relationship graph visualization (friends, family, rivals).

sections.description: Perspective-based information masking system that limits what Chronicler reveals to match what the player's civilization would realistically know: geographic, civilization, and individual scope masking; seven visibility caveats; database architecture; and phased rollout plan.

subsections.description: Geographic scope (known regions), civilization scope (allied/enemy civilizations), and individual scope (known HFs) masking dimensions.

Seven special-case visibility rules: organization membership propagation, nobles always visible, previous residence knowledge, starting dwarf background generation, family chain propagation, event-based revelation, LLM inference restrictions.
PostgreSQL view-based masking (not application-layer filtering) and phased rollout plan from full-data mode to progressively tighter scope masking.

sections.description: Multi-dimensional importance scoring for entities (figures, sites, conflicts, artifacts), Narrative Value Score for live denizens, rivalry detection, megabeast detection, and supernatural detection.

subsections.description: Computed importance scores for historical figures (df-narrator algorithm), sites, conflicts, and artifacts, stored in CDM for sorting and LLM context prioritization.
Per-dwarf narrative value score combining skill rarity, personality extremity, relationship depth, and event history for storytelling prioritization.
Co-appearance analysis to detect figure pairs with frequent adversarial event co-occurrence, flagging as rivals.
Flag historical figures as megabeasts (dragons, titans, etc.) or supernaturally cursed (vampires, werebeast, necromancers) based on type fields and event history.

sections.description: Universal UX patterns applied across all components: cross-linked entity references, perspective-aware event rendering, hover popovers, breadcrumb/prev-next navigation, and DF calendar system.

subsections.description: Every entity name anywhere in the UI is a hyperlink navigating to its detail page. Canonical UX pattern applied globally.
Event descriptions rendered from the perspective of the currently-viewed entity (e.g., 'was slain by' vs. 'slew').

aliases: REQ-VIS-011: Hover Popovers (Map and All Entity Links)
subsections.description: Summary card popovers on hover for all entity links and map markers.
Breadcrumb trail showing navigation history and prev/next controls for iterating through entity lists.
In-game DF calendar with months, seasons, and year formatting applied consistently across all date/time displays.

sections.description: Performance targets (XML parsing speed, explorer response time, graph rendering), security requirements (SQL injection prevention, credentials management), reliability (watcher uptime), scalability (multi-world), observability (storyteller logging), accessibility (search), and DF version compatibility.

subsections.description: XML parsing speed target, sub-second explorer page load target, and graph rendering performance target for large datasets.
Parameterized queries for all user input, credentials stored outside tracked files.
Watcher uptime/recovery requirements, multi-world data isolation, storyteller observability, search accessibility, and DF version compatibility constraints.

sections.description: Comprehensive DFHack technical reference: data access paths (Lua via dfhack-run), eventful module, repeat job pattern, state change hook, RemoteFortressReader (41 functions), and key gotchas (RPC broken for game-thread calls on DFHack 53.x).

subsections.description: All Lua data access paths available via dfhack-run over SSH, covering units, HFs, sites, items, artifacts, etc.
eventful module API for subscribing to in-game events (onDeath, onAttack, onJobInitiated, etc.).
Pattern for scheduling recurring Lua jobs via dfhack.timeout_active / repeat for bridge polling loops.
onStateChange hook for detecting game state transitions (game load, save, worldgen complete, etc.).
All 41 RFR RPC functions. Note: game-thread calls hang on DFHack 53.x under Prism; use dfhack-run SSH instead.
Known DFHack integration pitfalls: broken TCP RPC for game-thread calls, enable plugin behavior, SSH transport requirements.

sections.description: Full technology stack for Chronicler: Python backend (FastAPI/Flask), PostgreSQL 16 with extensions, Leaflet.js + D3.js + vis.js for visualization, LLM provider integration, DFHack Lua bridge, pgvector, and deployment tooling.

Current build status: what is implemented and working (CDM, XML parser, conversational Q&A, bridge v6, etc.) versus what is not yet started (Explorer UI, Visualizer, Mod Manager, Labor Manager, Advisor, etc.).

subsections.description: Components fully or substantially implemented as of the synthesis date.
Components not yet implemented, planned for future phases.

sections.description: Complete taxonomy of DF world event types: canonical reference source, merged event categories, and 19 event collection types. Covers 132+ event types used in narrative template system.

subsections.description: Master list of all DF legend event types with their XML tag names and field inventories.
Events grouped into semantic categories: combat, political, religious, cultural, supernatural, etc.
The 19 event collection container types (wars, raids, beasts, etc.) that group individual events.

sections.description: All canonical DF game data taxonomies referenced in design and implementation: HF fields, site types, entity types, biome types, structure types, HF relationship types, room types, furniture types, calendar system, material categories, personality facets, worship strength thresholds, and noble position categories.

aliases: HF_FIELDS -- Canonical HF Reference Field List
subsections.description: Canonical list of all HF data fields available from legends XML and DFHack bridge.
All 24 DF site types with their internal identifiers and display names.
All civilization/entity types in DF legends (dwarven, elven, human, goblin, kobold, etc.).
10 biome classification types used in site and region data.
12+ structure types found within sites (temples, market, library, guildhall, etc.).
All HF relationship type strings (spouse, parent, child, deity, master, slave, etc.).
22 constructable room types and 28 furniture types used in construction planning (MC-4 Advisor).
DF calendar structure: tick counts, months, seasons, year length conversion formulas.
Material category taxonomy from myDFHackScripts used in resource management and construction planning.
All 50 DF personality facets (beliefs, values, traits) with their internal names and display labels.
Numeric thresholds for DF worship strength categories (pray, sacrifice, etc.).
Categories of noble positions in DF (fortress nobility, entity nobility, etc.) used by Labor Manager and Advisor.

sections.description: Reusable software design patterns extracted from reference repositories and applied in Chronicler: event rendering pipeline, post-parse processing pipeline, exclusive action queue, polling+events hybrid, generic watcher factory, and bridge data flow architecture.

subsections.description: Pipeline pattern for rendering raw event records into human-readable narrative strings: fetch -> enrich -> template/LLM -> format.
10-step sequential pipeline run after XML parsing completes: relationship resolution, importance scoring, NVS, rivalry, embeddings, etc.
Queue pattern ensuring only one DFHack action executes at a time, preventing race conditions in AI advisor.
Combine periodic polling for bulk state with event subscriptions for immediate reaction to discrete events.
Factory pattern for creating typed game-state watchers that detect changes and emit typed events.

sections.description: The seven development phases with all stages and tasks, representing the chronological implementation plan. Phases are: Data Foundation, Explorer Core, Narrative Engine, Visualization, Live Integration, Advanced Components, and Polish & Production.

subsections.description: CDM schema extensions, XML parser completion, post-parse processing pipeline, test suite extension.
Entity detail page framework, primary and secondary entity detail pages, search and navigation.
Event narrative template system, narrative enrichment, agentic storyteller, monitoring and observability.
World map, charts and demographics, genealogy and network graphs, advanced visualizations.
Bridge enhancements, worldgen monitoring, knowledge horizon implementation.
Mod Manager core, Labor Manager core, AI Fortress Advisor core, plus deferred advanced features for all three.
Performance optimization, comprehensive test suite, packaging/deployment, and documentation.

sections.description: Priority mapping (P1-P4 labels mapped to phases), milestone definitions (v0.5, v1.0, v2.0 criteria), and risk register (technical risks and mitigations).

Prioritized action item list from research synthesis: Tier 1 Critical (blocks Narrative Engine/Explorer), Tier 2 High Value (visualization, data completeness), Tier 3 Bridge & Live Data Enhancements, Tier 4 Stretch/Advanced Features.
Component research reports, repository research reports, and planning & design documents that informed the research synthesis.
Content that does not fit existing categories. Currently empty -- all topics from the three source documents have been accommodated in the taxonomy above.

structural_decisions: The six Main Components (MC-1 through MC-6) and two Foundational Systems (FS-1, FS-2) from the PRD are preserved as top-level taxonomy nodes (sections 3-10) since they represent the fundamental product decomposition agreed upon across all three documents.

The Phase Roadmap (sections 21.1-21.7) is kept as a separate node even though it overlaps thematically with the component sections, because the roadmap organizes topics by implementation sequence rather than functional domain.

Cross-cutting concerns (Knowledge Horizon, Scoring, Navigation/UX, Non-Functional Requirements) are given dedicated top-level sections (11-14) rather than being folded into individual components, matching their 'cross-cutting' designation in both PRD and Research Synthesis.

DFHack Integration Reference (section 15) is preserved as a standalone section since it is a reference guide rather than a feature specification, even though its content informs ETL (section 4) and the Advisor (section 8).

Architectural Patterns (section 20) from Research Synthesis are preserved separately from the components they serve, because they represent reusable design knowledge extracted from reference repos that applies across multiple components.
Reference Data & Game Taxonomies (section 19) covers pure DF game data (site types, entity types, biomes, etc.) distinct from Chronicler product features, warranting its own top-level section.

The Research Synthesis 'Landscape Overview' and 'Reference Repository Inventory' are placed under section 1 (Product Vision & Overview) as they provide the research context that motivates the product vision.

REQ-VIS-011 (Hover Popovers on Map AND entity links) appears in both section 5.17 (Visualizer map context) and section 13.3 (Cross-Cutting UX) with the cross-cutting UX section being the canonical home, since the requirement spans both map and non-map contexts.

The 'Feature Inventory Summary' from Research Synthesis (a consolidated feature table) is placed under section 2.3 (Product Architecture) as a view across all components rather than a section of its own.

Consolidated Action Items (section 23) from Research Synthesis is kept as its own section because it represents a prioritized backlog view cutting across all components, useful for planning independently of the component taxonomy.

Sources & References (section 24) from Research Synthesis is preserved as-is since it documents the research methodology and provenance of the synthesis, which is metadata about the document set itself.
